h2h_summary: Belgium (most likely Winner Group G, FIFA rank 8) have a narrow all-time edge over Norway (most likely best 3rd-place qualifier from Group I), winning 1 and drawing 1 of their 2 documented senior international meetings, with Belgium never having lost to Norway in available records.
key_players_home: Kevin De Bruyne (attacking midfielder, creative engine, 34, exceptional range of passing)
Romelu Lukaku (centre-forward, Belgium all-time top scorer with 89 goals)
Thibaut Courtois (goalkeeper, Real Madrid, two-time Champions League winner)
Jeremy Doku (left winger, explosive dribbler, Manchester City)
Amadou Onana (defensive midfielder, Aston Villa, physical and combative)
key_players_away: Erling Haaland (centre-forward, Man City, 55 goals in 48 Norway caps, 16 goals in qualifying)
Martin Odegaard (attacking midfielder, Arsenal captain, creative playmaker)
Antonio Nusa (right winger, RB Leipzig, 21 years old, pace and dribbling)
Sander Berge (defensive midfielder, Fulham, shields the backline)
Alexander Sorloth (centre-forward, Atletico Madrid, physically imposing backup striker)
team_style_home: Belgium under Rudi Garcia deploy a 4-2-3-1 possession-based system with quick counter-attacks, De Bruyne dictating from central areas, Doku providing pace and width on the left, and an emphasis on collective spirit over data-driven tactics.
team_style_away: Norway under Stale Solbakken use a 4-3-3 built around aggressive high pressing, fast vertical transitions, and Haaland as the central reference point for diagonal runs and aerial duels, with Odegaard finding pockets between lines and full-backs pushing wide to create space.
wc_history_home: Belgium reached their best World Cup finish in 2018 with third place in Russia, defeating England 2-0 in the third-place playoff, and have appeared at 15 World Cups in total including fourth place at Mexico 1986.
wc_history_away: Norway return to the World Cup for the first time since 1998, when they famously defeated reigning champions Brazil 2-1 in the group stage before losing to Italy in the Round of 16; they qualified for 2026 with a perfect 8W-0D-0L record scoring 37 goals.
group_context: Group G (Belgium, Egypt, Iran, New Zealand) is one of the more straightforward groups with Belgium as heavy favourites to top it; the best 3rd-place team from Groups A/E/H/I/J is most likely Norway from Group I (France, Norway, Senegal, Iraq), who face a tough group but possess a golden generation capable of finishing third ahead of Iraq and then ranking as the best 3rd among their pool.
strengths_home: World-class creative midfield anchored by De Bruyne capable of unlocking any defence with incisive passing
Dynamic attacking combination of Doku's pace on the wing and Lukaku's aerial and physical presence
Experienced squad with Champions League and top European league pedigree and third-place World Cup finish in 2018
strengths_away: Erling Haaland is the most feared striker in the tournament, averaging a goal every 44 minutes in qualifying with elite movement and finishing
Norway recorded the highest-ever European World Cup qualifying goal tally (37 goals in 8 games) with a cohesive high-press system
Odegaard and Nusa provide creative and pace-based attacking threats alongside Haaland that can expose defensive lines on the counter
